risk_title: Governance Fragility Affecting Land Administration Implementation
risk_summary: The program's land administration activities depend on government institutional capacity and political stability. The document notes that the Rural Land Administration and Use (RLAU) Proclamation approval was delayed (originally due Dec 2019, approved May 2024), and institutional coordination challenges persist despite progress.
evidence_quote: The Rural Land Administration and Use Proclamation was approval to the Council of Ministers (CoM) in June 2023 and was approved by Parliament in May 2024.

risk_title: Land Tenure Disputes and Court Referrals
risk_summary: A significant number of land-related grievances are being referred to courts rather than resolved through program mechanisms, indicating potential underlying land tenure conflicts and governance challenges that could escalate.
evidence_quote: 1,881 cases under RA#2 have been recorded, of which 1,358 resolved and 523 referred to the court

risk_title: Incomplete Environmental and Social Risk Mitigation in Watershed Associations
risk_summary: Occupational health, safety, and gender-based violence prevention standards have only been incorporated into approximately 50% of Watershed User Cooperative Society bylaws, creating potential for social tensions and exclusion in community-based natural resource management.
evidence_quote: OHS provisions and standards of non-discrimination and GBV prevention have been incorporated into about 50% of WSUC bylaws, and the remaining will be addressed before the next CALM mission

risk_summary: The national Grievance Redress Mechanism is not fully recording all complaints submitted and resolved, indicating potential governance and accountability gaps that could undermine community trust and program legitimacy.
evidence_quote: The national GRM is functional but is currently not fully recording all complaints submitted and resolved.
